# Calculating line slope

## Short Teaching Summary
Calculating line slope: The slope of a line is the quotient of the length of the vertical side of the slope triangle and the length of the horizontal side of the slope triangle. Map connection: this idea links by requires to nearby concepts. Pause point: say the main idea in one short phrase before moving on.

## Difficulty
0.00

- Slope is rate of change

- Slope is rate of change (requires)

- The slope of a line is the quotient of the length of the vertical side of the slope triangle and the length of the horizontal side of the slope triangle.
